scenario: Tessera Bank, a bank operating 3 Self-Managed Camunda 8.8 clusters, evaluates how to run a set of custom outbound connector functions in production. The platform team wants to know which runtime options Camunda lists for self-managed deployments and what specific role SaaS plays in the same picture, because the platform engineers do not want to write their own wrapper unless the pre-packaged options do not fit the use case.
question: Which combination correctly describes the runtime environment options for Camunda 8.8 connectors?

options.text: On SaaS each cluster runs a built-in runtime; on Self-Managed the only available option is to build a custom runtime environment from scratch, with no packaged runtime artifact shipped by the SDK

On both SaaS and Self-Managed the runtime is part of the Zeebe broker process, so connector functions are invoked by the broker directly and no separate runtime environment is required for either deployment model

On SaaS clusters run a built-in runtime component; on Self-Managed the user must always deploy the same Docker runtime image, while the Spring Boot Starter is only intended for unit tests and local laptops

On SaaS each cluster runs a runtime component that knows the OOTB connectors; on Self-Managed the user provides the runtime via the Spring Boot Starter, the Docker runtime image, or a Custom runtime environment

a.text: Incorrect. The docs explicitly list a Spring Boot Starter and a Docker runtime image as packaged Self-Managed options alongside the custom runtime route.

b.text: Incorrect. The connector runtime is a separate component from the Zeebe broker; Self-Managed deployments must explicitly provide one of the runtime options.

c.text: Incorrect. The Spring Boot Starter is a first-class production option, not a tests-only artifact, and Self-Managed users are not restricted to the Docker image.

d.text: Correct. The docs say that in Camunda 8 SaaS every cluster runs a runtime component that knows the available out-of-the-box connectors and how to invoke them, and that for Self-Managed there are several options provided by Camunda: the Spring Boot Starter runtime, the Docker runtime image, and a Custom runtime environment.

explanation: Camunda 8.8 SaaS clusters bundle the connector runtime so users do not provision one. For Self-Managed installations the SDK lists three runtime options: the Spring Boot Starter runtime, the Docker runtime image, and a Custom runtime environment for use cases not covered by the first two.
